name: Ella Sophia Dunford
ai_summary: Ella Sophia Dunford has experience as a dental assistant and youth tennis coach working with children aged 7–13 in medical and recreational settings. Located in Salt Lake City with a 25-mile drive radius, she prioritizes safety and calm de-escalation during conflicts and creates supportive environments for social-emotional growth.

transcripts.q3: Hi, my name is Ella Sofia, Dunford and I have worked with children in both a medical and recreational setting. I worked as a dental assistant and I often worked with kids and helping them feel comfortable in a dental office. Explaining what the procedure would be, what was expected of them, helping them feel safe in that environment. And then I also had the opportunity to work as a youth tennis coach. So, I would work Oftentimes one-on-one with students. And then also in group settings, I'm helping them build new skills and build confidence. And those children were usually between the ages of 7 and 13. I really enjoy working with children, something. I've always enjoyed even from a young age, working with my younger siblings watching their growth and development is so rewarding, and then also getting to help a child build new skills and build confidence. Be so inspiring and children, they bring a lot of energy, a lot of honesty which keeps the day, exciting and engaging.
transcripts.q4: I am currently located in the st. George Utah area, but I will be moving to Salt Lake City Utah in August and I'm willing to drive up to 25 minutes to a shift.
transcripts.q5: During the summer months, I am looking to work on a full-time basis and I have a lot more availability, but then after I relocate to Salt Lake City in August, I would prefer to work part-time and my availability will largely depend on my class schedule. As I will be a full-time student at that time as well.
transcripts.q6: If one child were to hit another child, the first priority would definitely be safety calmly. Separating, the two children, and then, checking on the child, that was hit. Assessing how they are feeling and then going over to, to talk to the child who had and addressing the behavior in a calm way. But in a firm way, letting them know that their behavior is not. OK. And then helping the children Express their feelings. Both the child that was hurt in the child that hit helping them know that if there is a conflict in the future, they can use their words or they can ask for help in resolving conflict. But violence is never, the answer to resolving conflict and helping the two children understand that. And then also find a way to a resolution either by an apology being said and then accepted and then helping the children play again, together safely while monitoring the situation and help. Reinforce positive behavior from both of the children in the future.
transcripts.q7: I would make a great up kid teacher because I am patient positive and I really enjoy helping young kids learning grow. And I also understand at that age, kids are still developing their social and emotional skills. So I really focus on creating a safe and supportive environment where kids can feel comfortable exploring and expressing themselves.
